content_markdown: A fire engine with its siren sounding approaches and passes a stationary observer. The frequency of the sound emitted by the siren is $f_s$. The frequency of the sound measured by the observer is $f_o$. Which of the following describes the relationship between $f_o$ and $f_s$?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing the relationship between observed frequency and source frequency for a fire engine approaching and moving away from an observer.
labels_to_preserve: Fire engine moving away from observer

vibe_explanation: The Doppler effect states that when a source moves toward an observer, the wavefronts are compressed, leading to a higher observed frequency ($f_o > f_s$). Conversely, when the source moves away, the wavefronts are stretched out, leading to a lower observed frequency ($f_o < f_s$).
